description: Extend the Helm chart so all apps and services are deployable workloads. Today infra/helm/quant-platform/values.yaml only defines ws-gateway and triton-inference-server, and ingress.yaml only routes to ws-gateway.
steps: Add a workloads/services map entry in infra/helm/quant-platform/values.yaml for each app backend and each service (image, port, env, replicas, resources, health probes using @quant/health-server endpoints).
Parameterize templates (deployment.yaml, service.yaml) to iterate over the workloads map instead of hardcoding ws-gateway.
Extend ingress.yaml to route each Next.js app frontend + its /api to the right backend service (host/path rules per app).
Wire env/secrets: DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, JWT_SECRET/JWT_AUDIENCE, S3, NATS, KAFKA_BROKERS, LIVEKIT_* via Helm values + k8s Secrets (reference, do not commit secrets).
Validate: `helm template infra/helm/quant-platform` renders for all workloads with no errors; `helm lint` passes.
acceptance_criteria: values.yaml lists every app backend + service as a workload with probes and resources
Deployment/Service templates render all workloads (not just ws-gateway)
Ingress routes every app frontend + API
helm lint + helm template succeed with zero errors
